ucs.title: [LLM] Ember Bear / Cadet Blizzard WhisperGate-style wiper staging via Discord CDN + MBR/disk overwrite
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic two-stage WhisperGate (a.k.a. PAYWIPE/SHADYLOOK) deployment chain: PowerShell download from Discord CDN followed by .NET-loader abuse (InstallUtil/AddInProcess/RegAsm) and direct write to \\.\PhysicalDrive0. This is the destructive playbook GRU Unit 29155 used against Ukrainian government and has since reused against NATO logistics and critical infrastructure targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as ps_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*cdn.discordapp.com*" OR Processes.process="*discordapp.com/attachments*" OR Processes.process="*discord.com/api*") AND (Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*IWR *" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*Net.WebClient*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as wipe_cmd values(Processes.process_name) as wipe_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("InstallUtil.exe","AddInProcess.exe","RegAsm.exe","RegSvcs.exe") OR Processes.process="*\\\\.\\PhysicalDrive0*" OR Processes.process="*Set-Content*PhysicalDrive*" OR Processes.process="*[IO.File]::WriteAllBytes*PhysicalDrive*") by Processes.dest _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host _time as wipe_time] | where wipe_time>=firstTime AND (wipe_time-firstTime)<3600 | table firstTime,wipe_time,host,user,parent_process_name,ps_cmd,wipe_proc,wipe_cmd
ucs.kql:
let discord_stage = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("cdn.discordapp.com","discordapp.com/attachments","discord.com/api")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Invoke-WebRequest","IWR ","DownloadFile","DownloadString","Net.WebClient","curl ","wget ")
| project StageTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, StageCmd=ProcessCommandLine, StageParent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let wipe_stage = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName in~ ("InstallUtil.exe","AddInProcess.exe","RegAsm.exe","RegSvcs.exe") and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"))
   or ProcessCommandLine has @"\\.\PhysicalDrive0"
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)WriteAllBytes\(.{0,40}PhysicalDrive"
| project WipeTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, WipeProc=FileName, WipeCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
discord_stage
| join kind=inner (wipe_stage) on DeviceId
| where WipeTime between (StageTime .. StageTime + 1h)
| project StageTime, WipeTime, DeviceName, AccountName, StageParent, StageCmd, WipeProc, WipeCmd
| order by StageTime desc
ucs.rationale: The Discord-CDN-hosted .NET loader → MBR overwrite chain is the signature WhisperGate behavior CISA, Microsoft (Cadet Blizzard advisory), and Mandiant (UNC2589) attribute almost exclusively to GRU Unit 29155 / Ember Bear. Few legitimate enterprise workflows pull a binary from cdn.discordapp.com via PowerShell and then execute via InstallUtil with raw physical-drive writes inside the same hour, so

ucs.title: [LLM] Ember Bear post-exploitation: ntdsutil/VSS NTDS.dit theft chained with Impacket wmiexec lateral movement
ucs.description: Hunts the GRU Unit 29155 hands-on-keyboard pattern observed after they exploit an internet-facing app (Log4Shell, Confluence/Atlassian, Exchange, GoAnywhere): drop a webshell, dump credentials, then pivot DC-to-DC using Impacket's wmiexec/smbexec and exfiltrate ntds.dit via ntdsutil 'ifm' or VSS shadow copies. The characteristic Impacket wmiexec command-line artefact (cmd.exe /Q /c ... 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<epoch>) plus same-host ntds extraction is the hallmark of this crew, e.g. their 2022-2024 NATO-logistics intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as procs values(Processes.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( (Processes.process_name="ntdsutil.exe" AND Processes.process="*ifm*" AND Processes.process="*create full*") OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*create shadow*") OR (Processes.process="*\\windows\\ntds\\ntds.dit*") OR (Processes.parent_process_name="WmiPrvSE.exe" AND Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND Processes.process="*/Q /c*" AND Processes.process="*\\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$*") OR (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND Processes.process="*1> \\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\__*") ) by Processes.dest Processes.user host Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval impacket=if(match(cmds,"\\\\\\\\127\.0\.0\.1\\\\ADMIN\$\\\\__"),1,0), ntds=if(match(cmds,"(?i)(ntdsutil|vssadmin\s+create\s+shadow|ntds\.dit)"),1,0) | stats sum(impacket) as impacket_hits sum(ntds) as ntds_hits min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(cmds) as cmds values(procs) as procs values(users) as users by dest | where impacket_hits>0 AND ntds_hits>0 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let win = 24h;
let ntds_dump = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ifm" and ProcessCommandLine has "create full")
   or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create shadow")
   or (ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\windows\\ntds\\ntds\.dit")
   or (FileName =~ "esentutl.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ntds.dit")
| project NtdsTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, NtdsCmd=ProcessCommandLine, NtdsActor=AccountName;
let impacket = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "WmiPrvSE.exe" and FileName =~ "cmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/Q /c" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\\\127\.0\.0\.1\\ADMIN\$\\__\d+\.\d+")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)1>\s*\\\\127\.0\.0\.1\\ADMIN\$\\__\d+"
   or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "services.exe" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)cmd\.exe\s+/Q\s+/c.+\\\\.+\\C\$\\__output")
| project ImpTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ImpCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ImpActor=AccountName;
let webshell = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","php-cgi.exe","nginx.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe")
| project ShellTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ShellParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, ShellCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
ntds_dump
| join kind=inner (impacket) on DeviceId
| where ImpTime between (NtdsTime - win .. NtdsTime + win)
| join kind=leftouter (webshell) on DeviceId
| project DeviceName, NtdsTime, ImpTime, ShellTime, NtdsActor, NtdsCmd, ImpCmd, ShellParent, ShellCmd
| order by NtdsTime desc
ucs.rationale: Joint CISA/NSA/FBI advisory AA24-249A on Unit 29155 and Microsoft's Cadet Blizzard reporting both call out Impacket (wmiexec/smbexec) and ntdsutil-based NTDS theft, frequently launched from a JSP/PHP/ASPX webshell dropped after exploitation of public-facing apps (T1190) — exactly Ember Bear's documented chain. The Impacket wmiexec command line (cmd.exe /Q /c ... 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<epoch>.<fr
